# Services and Integrations

The `services/` tree contains the adapters that make the agent useful outside the local process.

## Anthropic API

`services/api/claude.ts` is the low-level request and streaming layer. It handles:
- model/provider selection
- streaming responses
- retries and error translation
- tool/result normalization
- usage and cost accounting

This is the transport layer that sits underneath `QueryEngine.ts`.

## MCP

The MCP integration provides server discovery, connection management, authentication, and resource proxying. It is the mechanism that lets the agent use external tool servers without hardcoding them into the core runtime.

## Analytics and Telemetry

The app uses analytics and telemetry layers for feature-gate checks, event logging, and session instrumentation. These services also influence prompt caching and runtime decisions in a few places.

## OAuth and Remote Modes

OAuth and remote-control paths live in `services/oauth/`, `bridge/`, and related helpers. These layers let the agent participate in authenticated or remote sessions without collapsing the core CLI design.

## Plugins and Skills

The project has first-class plugin and skill loading. That means capabilities can be extended without rewriting the core loop, which is important for keeping the system modular.
